commonIssues:
If the death is registered more than a year after the event, a Non-Availability Certificate (NAC) is required, initiating a complex process involving the RDO, Tahsildar (MRO), and VRO for verification.

How to fix:

  • Obtain a Non-Availability Certificate (NAC) from your local Municipality or Gram Panchayat first.
  • Submit an application with the NAC to the Revenue Divisional Officer (RDO) along with a Notarised Affidavit.
  • The RDO will direct the Tahsildar/MRO and VRO for field verification — cooperate promptly.
  • Apply via AP MeeSeva Portal or visit your nearest MeeSeva centre.
  • Helpline: 1800-425-2977 (AP Civil Supplies Toll-Free)

Applicants must provide a doctor's certificate, Panchanama, and other documents. Obtaining these for rural or home deaths can be particularly difficult.

How to fix:

  • For home deaths, obtain a certificate from a Registered Medical Practitioner (RMP) who attended the deceased.
  • Request a Panchanama from your local police station.
  • If no doctor was present, contact the local Primary Health Centre (PHC) for a post-mortem certificate.
  • Submit all documents at the nearest MeeSeva Centre.

Mistakes in name, date, or place of death require a separate, often slow, correction process involving notary affidavits and Gazetted Officers.

How to fix:

  • Prepare a Notary Affidavit on non-judicial stamp paper clearly stating the error and the correct information.
  • Get the affidavit attested by two Gazetted Officers.
  • Submit the affidavit and original certificate to the Registrar's office for correction.
  • Track correction status at AP MeeSeva Portal.

Users sometimes face technical issues at MeeSeva centers, especially when converting Category B (late registration) applications to Category A for final issuance.

How to fix:

  • Visit an alternate MeeSeva Centre in a nearby town/city if your local centre is experiencing technical problems.
  • Ask the MeeSeva operator to escalate the Category B to A conversion to their supervisor.
  • If the issue persists, contact the AP e-Governance Authority helpline: 1800-599-4788.
  • Raise a grievance at AP PDS/Civil Supplies Portal.

For non-natural deaths, families face hurdles in obtaining the Post Mortem Report and Form-2 from police authorities, delaying certificate issuance.

How to fix:

  • Request the Post Mortem Report (PMR) from the Government Hospital where the autopsy was conducted — it is your legal right.
  • Obtain Form-2 (Informant's Report) from the local police station handling the case.
  • Submit the PMR and Form-2 along with the FIR copy to the local civil registrar.
  • If police delay issuing documents, file a written complaint to the Superintendent of Police (SP) of your district.
